message:
feat: HITL step mode, robot-stress command, and per-agent health reporting

Implements csells feature requests from PR #13:

- session robot --step: runs ONE round then exits, enabling human-in-the-loop
  corrections between rounds. Resumes from prior session_state.json automatically.
- session robot --operator-notes <path>: injects operator corrections into
  round prompts so agents integrate human feedback.
- session robot-stress: adversarial stress test that runs all 3 agents against
  surviving hypotheses with a "kill the survivors" mandate. Supports
  --operator-context for domain-specific guidance.
- Per-agent health reporting in JSON output: surfaces individual agent failures
  (rate limits, timeouts, no output) in structured data per round.
- Gemini CLI switched from --sandbox to --yolo for full tool access (web search,
  file reads), matching Claude (--dangerously-skip-permissions) and Codex
  (--full-auto). Per csells: "let Gemini off the leash."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
